allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps tier-2-high-value distinguish real leverage from isolated local wins by showing interdependencies and pattern change.
allies.source_quote: Systems Thinking provides the broad conceptual framework for seeing interrelationships and patterns of change, enabling the identification of the true high-leverage points.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Tier-2-high-value conflicts with abstraction when high-level framing drifts away from the operational evidence needed to verify leverage.
structured_tensions.source_quote: tier-2-high-value conflicts with abstraction (abstraction) when high-level framework thinking drifts from the concrete, operational evidence required to validate whether the identified high-value drivers are real.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Tier-2-high-value conflicts with leverage-points when pruning to a few drivers hides systemic interdependencies that require simultaneous intervention.
structured_tensions.source_quote: tier-2-high-value conflicts with leverage-points (leverage points) when focusing on a few high-value factors obscures systemic interdependencies that require simultaneous intervention at multiple leverage points.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
